experience: Joining the pre-dawn fish auction (asta del pesce) at the Catania fish market, La Pescheria, where buyers and sellers conduct bidding in rapid Sicilian dialect beginning around 5am, followed by eating granita con brioche at a nearby bar as the sun rises
why_distinctive: Most tourists visit La Pescheria after 8am when it becomes a tourist spectacle. The actual wholesale auction hour — documented by food writers as a separate, participatory world — involves theatrical auctioneer chanting and swordfish being carried on men's shoulders. The granita ritual immediately after is the authentic local breakfast pairing.
source: Mary Taylor Simeti, 'Pomp and Sustenance: Twenty-Five Centuries of Sicilian Food' (1989); also documented in Melissa Muller's reporting on Sicilian markets for Saveur magazine
